one_liner: Snowflake is a cloud data platform offering REST/SQL APIs and Cortex AI services, authenticated via OAuth, key-pair JWT, PATs, or workload identity federation, with a self-serve free trial and an official managed MCP server.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth token flow; used for REST API, SQL API, and MCP server access with role-based access control.
Programmatic Access Tokens (PAT) generated by Snowflake; passed as Authorization: Bearer <token> with X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN.
Key pair authentication using locally generated JWT signed with private key (RSA), and Workload Identity Federation using cloud provider tokens.

self_serve_notes: Free trial account available via self-service signup form without payment info required; full feature access during trial. Converts to paid self-service account when payment method is added. Developers can generate key pairs, PATs, or OAuth tokens themselves without needing special admin approval for basic API access, though some enterprise auth setups (OAuth integrations) may need admin/ACCOUNTADMIN privileges.

api_surface.notes: Snowflake REST API (OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec) for resource management, plus SQL API for executing statements, and Cortex APIs (Analyst, Search, Agents). Broad surface covering data warehouse operations, account/resource management, and AI services.

evidence.supports: Describes four auth methods: key pair, OAuth, PAT, workload identity federation, with header formats.
SQL API authentication via OAuth, key-pair JWT, and workload identity federation, with example CLI commands.
Programmatic access tokens (PAT) as a self-serve auth method.
Free trial account self-service signup without payment info, full feature access.
REST API compliant with OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec for resource management.

flags: Snowflake-managed MCP server is officially supported (revision 2025-11-25), exposing Cortex Analyst, Cortex Search, Cortex Agents, and SQL execution tools via CREATE MCP SERVER command.
Community-run MCP projects are deprecated in favor of the official managed service.
Some OAuth integration setups may require ACCOUNTADMIN privileges, which could be a caveat for fully self-serve agent deployments in enterprise accounts.
